rows: Avalon Hotel and Bungalows Palm Springs

They tried to charge me twice the amount I owed for my stay, even though I fully paid in advanced through my credit card company. They did issue a refund, but only because we called twice to have it taken care of. Then I was also overcharged for the amenity fee, which was supposed to have been included in my first payment.Second, the building is sketchy if you’re not in the main part of the hotel. Our walk and view was of an empty small alley way, where they parked the hotels cars. It was loud and you could hear every car.Also, the room was empty in a way that made it feel desolate and non-inviting. It has weird textured walls  and odd green carpet. The bathroom smells like grapefruits.Definitely wouldn’t recommend here if you’re staying near Beverly Hills.

Good: Its almost painful to give a bad review to this hotel because there are genuinely some very nice staff members - mainly the valet staff.Bad: Unfortunately the hotel is not clean at all. I had to ask them to clean my room every day as house keeping either forgot or ran out of time. The suites building across the street is overlooked by staff. Avoid booking those suites, they have zero natural sunlight which makes depressing to stay in. The pictures of the pool area do NOT represent reality.

Made a reservation. After driving many hours me and my son arrived at 3pm. Only to be treated rudely and told we need to figure out what to do with our car while we waited for our room to be ready. Hour later still no room and no communication as to how long it would be. No apologies, no offers for drinks while we waited. Just go figure out what to do with your car even after we paid for everything including valet parking. The attitude of the staff was as though we were a bother to them. Not once did we ever get even an estimate of how long we need to wait. "We'll call you when it's ready" was the only thing we were told. I've stayed at motel 6 and was treated better. Our entire evening was wasted on vacation waiting for a room we reserved.

Good: The staff were great! Very professional and personable. The design of the hotel was retro and stylish and rich in history.Bad: The rooms are stylish but not the most functional, especially the toilet (no counter space, the window/circulation window opens into the corridor). You're paying for the hip factor/design, essentially.I think the rooms work for a single person or a couple (at best). Didn't work so well as a family hotel.

A Motel in BH. You’re paying 70% to the name BH.Prison-like hallway, zero soundproofing walls. On the first night 2AM I heard someone scanning the room card, and I thought someone was trying to open our room door, but really that was just our next door coming back. Absolutely terrible experience to feel safe to sleep.Valet is not available after hour as well. Too bad the front desk didn’t tell us so we had to park ourselves with the same $60 parking fee.

after flying all night on a red eye. We  arrived and they were unable to check us in had to call manager I had already communicated via text with them our flight arrives at 5:30am they were still unprepared.

Stayed for 3 nights and I’m glad it wasn’t for longer because the walls are paper thin and not only that if you don’t stay in the top floor be prepared to hear loud non-stop stepping at night if someone’s in the room above you.. not their fault but rather the building itself, for the prices charged this is crazy. 300$+ a night..? And ontop of that, there are hidden fees they charge you without any mention on arrival or on booking, so be prepared to get charged 40$+ a night on top of your stay upon check out
Good: 真的太一般了，停车默认收费，其实完全可以自己去停的，他们根本不给你选择。直接收取65USD一晚。周边所有停车场都在USD25-35之间！Bad: 其次，楼层味道发霉的味道。一进去房间扑鼻而来的发霉味道！

The Avalon was simply “ok”.  The bar closed at 9 pm on a Friday so impossible to get a drink after dark and not within walking distance of anything.  The people and service were the bright star but other than that the $65 parking fee and $41 “amenity fee” for literally no amenities other than a dirty pool were not even close to worth it.   Thank goodness for the Amex credits that made it somewhat affordable but without those it’s a total ripoff for a tolerable bed and paper thin walls.
The staff were understaffed for valet and bell hop. We were in a hurry to get to a major event, waited 40 minutes for our luggage and then went and retrieved it ourselves!

I arrived at 4:30pm and had filled out the check in form in advance, check in was at 3pm and at 4:30pm I was told room was not ready and they didn't know when it will be ready which was really annoying. I asked them to accomodate late checkout in that case and they rejected to accomodate a full late checkout at 4pm amd said i could checkout at 2pm. They said I was going to get free drinks (the VIP access mentioned also free drink per person per stay) but did not get jt. I also confirmed that I did not need valet parking as the street was full of parking spots. I got my room, the room was nice spacious and clean and checked out the next day. Afterwards I received a folio via email and I was charged for valet parking. I had to call them and tell them I was charged and they looked into it then reversed the charge. It is a great location, room was spacious but for the price paid horrible service. I had to run after them to get certain things done and I had to request late checkout when they failed to check me in on time.
Good: LocationBad: Old furniture and not clean

Das Hotel ist nah am Rodeo Drive und hat Fernlicht daher eine tolle Lage gehabt. Die Mitarbeiter waren auch nett und das Frühstück lecker.Location: Nah am Rodeo DriveService: Der Service war nett aber nichts besonderes.Food: Lecker aber auch hochpreisigerRoom: Großes Zimmer mit allem was man benötigt, relativ kleines Badezimmer.
Good: Shabby chic Room, great Pool and Restaurant, complimentary breakfast

I can’t say enough wonderful things about the Avalon Hotel and its staff. My favorite place to be was at the cabana by the pool with family (or working with a yummy breakfast). Even during colder months, that spot has heat lamps that make it the perfect place to be, morning or evening.The staff was always personable, helpful and welcoming. A shoutout to Carlos and Luis at the front desk for helping to extend my stay. And of course the waitstaff at Vivianne were fantastic as well.I only wish my stay could have been longer. I’ll be back. :)

I am so happy I got to go back not specifically on business travel! I don't want to leave in the morning.Apon arrive at 11:45 am I had 2 giant suitcases and at 12:15 pm lunch so I assumed I would check bags. The lovely dude (maybe Jeremy) said welcome back and we have upgraded your room. He gave me a ginger shot and 2 water cartons which was handy after a few conferences and flying a bunch with germs.The location is sooooo walkable and the restaurant, Viviane, is amazing. Addison and everyone else could not be more helpful....pouring rain/cold and you can sit outside by the beautiful pool under heat lamps and have cheerful service. The tuna poke bowl was amazing. Addison is from Hawaii and said it is the best in LA so super high praise.I had teams meetings all day with only two breaks. The guy at the front desk planned my room cleaning to coincide to follow my break with the woman who would clean who has been at the property for decades and her break.....so clean.The architecture and decor is beautiful and look forward to my next stay!
We went for a conference so I am not sure about the service and food offered.

Good: The staff was excellent. Every employee was cordial and friendly. The people at the counter, the restaurant staff, and the valet workers were all very nice.Bad: Cannot think of anything I didn't like.

I would highly recommend the Avalon Hotel for your stay.  My wife had to have emergency surgery on 9/23/25 and we live 3+ hours away.  I just happen to stumble across the Avalon Hotel while looking for a place to stay after surgery.  From the moment I called and explained my situation  the staff was very accommodating to her recover needs.  The staff here treated us like family and always asked if there was anything they could do for us.  We have been back 3 times after this first experience for follow up visit and know we make a weekend out of it.  I also want to mention they have a great restaurant on the premise.Special Thanks to the following: Front Desk:  Maxine, Maribel, Jeremy, Carlo, Luis Restaurant: George, Feruk, Tony, Walner, Jason, Chris, Pepe and special thanks to chef Jesus Valet: Emerson, Arturo, Carlos, Chris, Dion, Brad, Michael and Special Thanks to the Managers for making all this happen: Martin, Hudith and Addison.See you shortly  :)
The room, the people, the restaurant, are all perfect. We will always stay here.  We visit our family and always have the best time.

Good: RestaurantenBad: Rummet var ikke klar ved ankomst og først 2 timer efter tjek ind, hvilket ikke er godt, når man ankommer træt.Parkering var ikke gratis, hvilket jeg havde fået indtryk af.Der blev ikke gjort rent hver dag.

Good: L emplacement, le restaurant et le petit déjeuner   quel delice et personnel très sympathique et aux petits soins.Piscine et coins cosi autour un havre de paix.Super accueil à la réception.La TV XXL dans la chambre.Bad: A revoir les parties communes du batiment attenant, odeur dans l ascenseur et le couloir manque de propreté..
Had a lovely weekend stay at the Avalon. Great staff, groovy pool area and excellent restaurant experience. We would definitely return. Also a convenient location to lots of walkable shops

The Avalon was simply “ok”.  The bar closed at 9 pm on a Friday so impossible to get a drink after dark and not within walking distance of anything.  The people and service were the bright star but other than that the $65 parking fee and $41 “amenity fee” for literally no amenities other than a dirty pool were not even close to worth it.   Thank goodness for the Amex credits that made it somewhat affordable but without those it’s a total ripoff for a tolerable bed and paper thin walls.

Just had a great stay at this sweet little boutique hotel… very clean, groovy, amazing location, food, service, pool, and vibe. Note ** Carlos (in valet) was super cool, very accommodating, so sweet to my puppy, and a general all around good dude. Highly recommend this hotel 🤩

This is a boutique hotel in Beverly Hills, within walking distance of Rodeo Drive. The rooms are very spacious and immaculately kept.  There is a small but fully equipped gym, and the outdoor pool area is very inviting.  The food was so good that we ate most of our meals around the pool.  While not inexpensive, we found the food comparable to anywhere outside the hotel along Beverly Drive.  Everyone, front desk staff, wait staff, valet staff, is very friendly and accommodating. We were also pleasantly surprised about the price; we have paid more for less in Canada! So I would recommend the Avalon for value alone. But it offers that and more.  I would highly recommend this hotel and look forward to returning soon.

I’ve absolutely loved staying at the Avalon Hotel in Beverly Hills over the past year! The staff here are truly wonderful — everyone is so friendly, kind, and welcoming. The rooms are spacious and comfortable, and the gym is always clean and well-maintained.But what really makes this place special is the valet team — they are simply incredible. Super fast, attentive, and genuinely caring every single time. I especially want to thank Arturo and Carlos for always remembering me and treating me with so much warmth and respect. They truly go above and beyond!I’ve yet to try their restaurant so that’ll be next!The Avalon feels like a home away from home. Highly recommend!

The rooms are lovely and the service exceeds all expectations, but there is one tiny thing that I was not a fan of.  It is a 4-5 star "Hotel" that is more like a REALLY, REALLY nice motel.  I don't like having my door facing the outdoors...I prefer the door to my hotel room to lead to a hallway.With that in mind, EVERYTHING else was perfect!  The entire staff was warm, friendly and welcoming.  The room itself was awesome.  The food and cocktails at the bar/restaurant were spot on.  The chocolate in my room upon arrival was quite memorable.  The pool area was great.  I would absolutely stay here again!
Rooms little small but I love the vibe and the decor and design of the place. All Asop products. Restaurant for food is great and pool is cute. My favorite staff was Arturo and Carlos!! Great service

Special thanks to Carlos, Michael, Arturo, and Chris for their kindness to this solo parent traveling with kids internationally. They were so kind and predictive of what we needed - all had great recommendations on local options for food and shopping.  Truly grateful and they, along with all the Avalon staff, made our visit and stay memorable. Will absolutely be back when again in L.A..

The hotel was charming and nicely laid-out.  Every staff encounter was friendly and the front desk staff (Jeremy and Maribel)) were so helpful and warm.  Waitstaff and food were also great (best frittata I’ve ever had).  The bathrooms are small, but not a problem for us.

The room was spacious and comfortable, very walkable to the Golden Triangle and restaurants. Easy to take car shares to other spots in West Hollywood.Staff was excellent and friendly, with Maribel and Jeremy as standouts! Friendly, helpful and kind! Thank you for making my stay at the Avalon a great one!
Menu was awesome, I have diabetic restrictions and was able to select healthy choices from your menu. Thank you for taking that consideration to your menu
You should offer the fish tacos at dinner too.
I thought it was kind of overpriced for a smoothie with protein powder.
Our compliments to the chef(s)!  Everything we had to eat and drink was truly amazing.  The best hotel restaurant food we have had.
Every meal was excellent and the service was top notch
I have been staying at The Avalon since 2006 and the restaurant is now very very good , the menu is varied and the food is first class .
Excellent food and service, but you could expand lunch menu a little but with more options! :)
Need gluten free options.  Time to renovate the menus.  Need more healthy and delicious options.    Pepe has been there for years and is wonderful, friendly, efficient and professional.
I only had the complimentary coffee in the mornings which was below average in quality.
Menu is average and not remarkable. Just the basics. Motel like. Can play up a couple of steps and standards. Better ingredients product and technique
Would have been nice to have pastry complimentary.
Extremely pricey. $19.00 a bowl of oatmeal is ridiculous
The showers aren't great for women. You have to have a very strong shower cap if you want to NOT wash your hair since the shower head is directly overhead.
Only had wine and coffee given my stay was very short but appreciated the availability and vibe of the restaurant!
prices were on the high side; espresso coffee drink tasted burnt
The menu needs more fresh options and gluten free pasta.
In room service and food was great. Probably our favorite thing about this hotel.
Suggest adding a low fat milk and soda options to the on line menu.
Outrageously expensive for a pot of coffee ($30 after tip) when it’s being offered for free in the restaurant. I will never make that rookie move again!
My salad had a living bug in it. I wasn't brought water, no salt or petpper either.
My wife ordered a smoothie that she really liked.    Had wanted initially some Hot Tea but didn’t find it on the in room dining menu.

Ordered a cheese quesadilla with side salad;  cheese was congealed and greasy, really poor execution for a simple food and with room service 20% added to the bill it was a VERY  EXPENSIVE meal which was not worth the value.
Terrible salad  Would appreciate credit because it was left outside room uneaten
Food came super fast which was so nice and unlike other hotels where it takes upwards of 45 minutes.
Guest returned to room after 6hr to find the ac was not working, guest does not want to pay for room that has no ac
No room available to move to, said manaegment will reach out in the morning and i have notified eng manager. I gave guest the large far used at front desk and filled with water.
Front Office - not satisfied with room

Guest was originally booked for Beverly classic king but upon check in, after she was told her room was in the beverly building she demanded a room here in the main building. Saying last time she had the same issue and they still moved her
Guest wanted room in main building, Luis reassigned to deluxe king room. Guest came back down upset that her room didn't have a welcome amenity like her colleagues
She checked in after amenities were dropped, her amenity was in original room we will move it over to her new room guest said it would have been nice to be welcomed with that gift like colleagues
While room was being serviced it was noted that the room had a strong weed smell and there was smoking items left on patio

Upon arriving into room after check in guest came back down to inform me that the room she entered was dirty/ hasn't been cleaned. She said she left her suitcase in the closet and it is no big deal she is leaving for the day.
Guest returned to room around 10:30pm to find that the power in the bedroom did not work.
Offered guest new room for the night. He said he would stay and ask that it be looked at inthe morning  after 9:30am. Bathroom lights working

F&B servers informed me that Mr. Cohen arrived and asked to use Cabana 45, which he had reserved earlier that day. However, another guest was already occupying the cabana and had been ordering food and beverages. After reviewing the check and the F&B charges posted to the room, it was determined that the guest was Ms. Blumkin in Room 306.  This incident occurred at approximately 12:30 PM. Ms. Blumkin was asked to leave by Mr. Cohen directly and not by hotel staff. Ms. Blumkin had reserved the cabana in advance, while Mr. Cohen’s reservation was made the same day at around 10:00 AM.  I called Ms. Blumkin and left a voicemail to let her know I am trying to reach her to discuss the situation and gather additional information. The servers also informed me that Mr. Cohen was upset that the cabana he had reserved was already occupied and being used by another guest.
